examples.title: Scrum Sprint Planning Algorithm
examples.description: Demonstrates the sprint planning process in Scrum framework
examples.code:
SPRINT PLANNING
BEGIN
 INPUT: Product Backlog, Sprint Goal
 
 // Part 1: What can be delivered?
 PRINT "Sprint Planning Meeting Started"
 capacity = calculateTeamCapacity()
 velocity = getPreviousSprintVelocity()
 
 selectedItems = []
 FOR each item IN Product Backlog (ordered by priority)
 IF totalEffort(selectedItems) + item.effort <= capacity
 ADD item TO selectedItems
 IF size(selectedItems) >= velocity
 BREAK
 END IF
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Part 2: How will it be achieved?
 sprintBacklog = []
 FOR each item IN selectedItems
 tasks = decomposeIntoTasks(item)
 ADD tasks TO sprintBacklog
 END FOR
 
 sprint = CREATE Sprint(sprintBacklog, Sprint Goal)
 PRINT "Sprint Planning Completed"
 RETURN sprint
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the Sprint Planning ceremony in Scrum where the team selects items from the Product Backlog based on capacity and velocity, then decomposes them into tasks for the Sprint.

examples.title: Kanban Flow Control Algorithm
examples.description: Demonstrates WIP limiting and flow management in Kanban
examples.code:
KANBAN FLOW MANAGEMENT
BEGIN
 INPUT: Kanban Board, New Item
 
 // Add item to first available column
 firstColumn = getFirstColumn()
 ADD New Item TO firstColumn
 
 // Monitor and enforce WIP limits
 WHILE system is running
 FOR each column IN Kanban Board
 currentWIP = countItems(column)
 wipLimit = column.wipLimit
 
 IF currentWIP > wipLimit
 PRINT "Warning: WIP limit exceeded in", column.name
 notifyTeam()
 BLOCK new items from entering column
 ELSE
 ALLOW flow through column
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Move items through columns
 FOR each item IN columns (except Done)
 IF item.isReady() AND column.hasCapacity(item.nextColumn)
 MOVE item TO nextColumn
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 WAIT for time interval
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows how Kanban enforces WIP limits and manages the flow of work items through different workflow stages.

examples.title: Product Backlog Prioritization
examples.description: Demonstrates backlog prioritization using MoSCoW method
examples.code:
BACKLOG PRIORITIZATION
BEGIN
 INPUT: List of User Stories
 
 FUNCTION prioritizeBacklog(stories)
 mustHave = []
 shouldHave = []
 couldHave = []
 wontHave = []
 
 FOR story IN stories
 IF story.priority == "Must Have"
 ADD story TO mustHave
 ELSE IF story.priority == "Should Have"
 ADD story TO shouldHave
 ELSE IF story.priority == "Could Have"
 ADD story TO couldHave
 ELSE
 ADD story TO wontHave
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Sort within each category by business value
 SORT mustHave BY businessValue (DESC)
 SORT shouldHave BY businessValue (DESC)
 SORT couldHave BY businessValue (DESC)
 
 // Concatenate with must-have first
 prioritized = CONCATENATE(mustHave, shouldHave, couldHave, wontHave)
 
 RETURN prioritized
 END FUNCTION
 
 sortedBacklog = prioritizeBacklog(Product Backlog)
 RETURN sortedBacklog
END
examples.explanation: This demonstrates how Product Backlog items are prioritized using the MoSCoW method (Must, Should, Could, Won't) and sorted by business value within each category.
